rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component or implementation scopes
Place component type contracts in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use visible identifiers from the current scope or explicitly provided whitelist
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope that contains all referenced identifiers
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic in AGREE contracts
Use AGREE logical operators such as and, or, not(...), and implication => in contracts
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, for example 0.0 -> pre(t) + step
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree {** ... **}; blocks
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool in declarations
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use component ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
